vocab.example: Traditional art often focuses on aesthetic appreciation through realism and meticulous attention to detail.

vocab.meaning: kích thích trí tuệ, gợi suy nghĩ sâu xa
vocab.example: I find modern art to be more intellectually provocative as it challenges my preconceived notions of what art should be.

vocab.meaning: tay nghề thủ công tinh xảo
vocab.example: The reason I admire classical paintings is the exquisite craftsmanship evident in every single brushstroke.
Contemporary artists frequently use visual metaphors to convey complex abstract concepts like isolation or time.

vocab.meaning: tiên phong, phá cách, đi trước thời đại
vocab.example: While I respect the old masters, I have always been drawn to the avant-garde movements that push the boundaries of creativity.

vocab.meaning: gợi lên cảm giác hoài cổ
vocab.example: Traditional landscapes never fail to evoke a sense of nostalgia and tranquility in the viewer.
Traditional art is arguably more significant because these masterpieces have stood the test of time for centuries.

vocab.meaning: bình luận, phê phán xã hội
vocab.example: Many modern installations serve as powerful social commentary on pressing issues like climate change or inequality.

vocab.meaning: một sự tương phản rõ rệt
vocab.example: The minimalist style of modern galleries stands in stark contrast to the ornate frames found in traditional museums.

vocab.meaning: truyền tải những cảm xúc sâu sắc
vocab.example: Both styles aim to convey profound emotions, though they utilize vastly different visual languages to do so.
